structural_verification.evidence: All 6 Snapshot references traced, none type-discriminating. Downstream consumers select by session+seq only. Collapse from 2 effects to 1 per fill is safe.

proposal.claim: CEProcessFill and CEProcessRecoveredPending each produce an MFR+Snapshot pair atomically, consuming 2 sequence numbers per fill. The Snapshot carries no payload distinction from MFR in the spec (both are type-tagged records in ce_log with identical structure [type, seq, session]). No invariant or downstream action distinguishes MFR from Snapshot in its guards or cursor logic (OMSConsumeCE advances cursor by eff.seq = cursor+1 regardless of type, CEBackupApply applies by seq regardless of type, ArchiverIngestCE stores regardless of type). Collapsing MFR+Snapshot into a single MFR effect per fill halves the sequence-space cost of fills, reduces ce_log growth rate for fill-heavy workloads, and simplifies CEProcessFill and CEProcessRecoveredPending from 2-effect atomic production to 1-effect production, eliminating the ce_seq+1<=MaxSeq guard strengthening (only ce_seq<=MaxSeq needed).

judgment.ruling: Proposal is correct. No property is violated by collapsing the MFR+Snapshot pair to a single MFR effect per fill. The Snapshot type carries no semantic weight in the spec: it is never type-discriminated by any consumer, guard, or invariant. The guard simplification from ce_seq+1<=MaxSeq to ce_seq<=MaxSeq is correct and aligns fill actions structurally with CEProduceDirectEffect. Reviewer confidence 0.93 is warranted; judge concurs at 0.96. ACCEPT.

open_items.item: Snapshot remains in CEEffectTypes as a dead type. Removal from CEEffectTypes and from CEProduceDirectEffect exclusion sets (spec:223, spec:774) is a valid follow-up but out of scope for this step.
